name: NIST Cybersecurity Framework 2.0
publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
description: Six-function framework for managing and reducing cybersecurity risk. CSF 2.0 (Feb 2024) added GOVERN as the sixth function.

functions.name: Govern
functions.description: Establish and monitor the organization's cybersecurity risk management strategy, expectations, and policy.

categories.name: Organizational Context
categories.description: The circumstances — mission, expectations, dependencies, legal/regulatory — surrounding the cybersecurity risk decisions.

categories.name: Risk Management Strategy
categories.description: Risk management priorities, constraints, risk tolerance, and assumptions are established.

functions.name: Identify
functions.description: Develop an organizational understanding of cybersecurity risks to systems, people, assets, data, and capabilities.

functions.name: Protect
functions.description: Implement appropriate safeguards to ensure delivery of critical services.

categories.name: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control

functions.name: Detect
functions.description: Identify the occurrence of a cybersecurity event in a timely manner.

functions.name: Respond
functions.description: Take action regarding a detected cybersecurity incident.

functions.name: Recover
functions.description: Restore capabilities or services impaired by a cybersecurity incident.
